name: Porzuna from Los Cortijos – circular via El Robledo and the Río Bullaque
name_original: Porzuna desde Los Cortijos – circular por El Robledo y el río Bullaque
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Los Cortijos traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, valleys, and occasional mountain passes. The region offers diverse terrain, from routes winding through agricultural areas to those ascending into the Montes de Toledo. These paths provide varied gradients suitable for different cycling abilities, often featuring scenic views of the surrounding natural environment. Best road cycling routes around Los Cortijos The most popular road cycling route is Los Cortijos and Puerto de los Robledillos Loop, a 31.5 miles (50.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete, featuring a notable climb through Puerto de los Robledillos. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Montes de Toledo Circuit, a difficult 75.8 miles (122.0 km) path that explores the mountainous terrain of the Montes de Toledo. Local road cyclists also love the Consuegra, Urda y Puerto del Comendador — circular desde los Yébenes, a 61.3 miles (98.6 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including Puerto del Comendador, often completed in about 4 hours 12 minutes. Road cycling around Los Cortijos is defined by rolling hills, mountain passes, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging circuits. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 70 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Los Cortijos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Los Cortijos?
A: There are 6 dedicated road cycling routes around Los Cortijos listed on komoot, offering a range of experiences from moderate loops to challenging circuits. These routes traverse diverse terrain, from rolling hills to mountain passes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or less experienced riders in Los Cortijos?
A: While there are no routes explicitly marked as 'easy' in the guide, five of the six routes are rated as moderate. These routes, often winding through agricultural areas and valleys, can be enjoyed by riders with a reasonable fitness level. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366194974/los-cortijos-and-puerto-de-los-robledillos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cortijos and Puerto de los Robledillos Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a manageable distance with a notable climb.

Q: What are the most challenging road cycling routes in the Los Cortijos region?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371434336/montes-de-toledo-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo Circuit&lt;/a&gt; is rated as difficult. This extensive 75.8-mile (122.0 km) path explores the mountainous terrain of the Montes de Toledo, featuring significant elevation gains and demanding sections.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling around Los Cortijos?
A: The Los Cortijos region offers a varied landscape for road cycling. You'll encounter rolling hills, river valleys, and agricultural areas, with routes occasionally ascending into the Montes de Toledo. The region is characterized by its natural environment, providing scenic views throughout your ride.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for a full day's ride?
A: Yes, several routes are suitable for longer rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371434336/montes-de-toledo-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo Circuit&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 75.8-mile (122.0 km) route. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366199793/consuegra-urda-y-puerto-del-comendador-circular-desde-los-yebenes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra, Urda y Puerto del Comendador — circular desde los Yébenes&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 61.3 miles (98.6 km) and can take over 4 hours to complete.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Los Cortijos?
A: While the guide doesn't specify, regions with similar characteristics in Spain, like Andalusia, benefit from a warm climate year-round, making them ideal for cycling almost any time of the year. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Los Cortijos?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Los Cortijos are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366194974/los-cortijos-and-puerto-de-los-robledillos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cortijos and Puerto de los Robledillos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092411523/malagon-and-porzuna-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malagón and Porzuna Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092336692/porzuna-from-los-cortijos-circular-via-el-robledo-and-the-rio-bullaque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porzuna from Los Cortijos – circular via El Robledo and the Río Bullaque&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Los Cortijos?
A: Over 70 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Los Cortijos's varied terrain. While specific reviews aren't detailed here, the popularity of routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366194974/los-cortijos-and-puerto-de-los-robledillos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cortijos and Puerto de los Robledillos Loop&lt;/a&gt; suggests riders appreciate the combination of scenic beauty and engaging climbs, such as through Puerto de los Robledillos.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: While the specific routes in this guide don't detail historical points, the broader region of Los Cortijos and similar areas in Spain are known for routes that weave through areas rich in history and culture. Cyclists might encounter historic structures or charming villages, offering a glimpse into local life and traditions.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly road cycling in Los Cortijos?
A: The guide primarily features moderate to difficult road cycling routes. While there are no routes specifically labeled 'easy' or 'family-friendly,' families with older children or those comfortable with moderate distances and some elevation gain might enjoy sections of the less demanding moderate routes, especially those that pass through flatter agricultural areas.

Q: What are some of the best scenic viewpoints for road cyclists in Los Cortijos?
A: The routes around Los Cortijos offer numerous scenic views, particularly as they traverse rolling hills and ascend into higher elevations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366194974/los-cortijos-and-puerto-de-los-robledillos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cortijos and Puerto de los Robledillos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, features a notable climb through Puerto de los Robledillos, which would likely offer panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscape.
tips: Los Cortijos sits in the valley on the north side of the Sierra de los Montes de Toledo on the CR-713 road. The town is surrounded by mountains with incredible views.

El Robledo is located in a privileged natural setting in the valley where the Alcobilla and Bullaque rivers meet, in the Matagrande plain and between the Toledana and Piedralá plains, near Cabañeros National Park.

Cabañeros National Park is one of the most valuable protected areas of the Montes de Toledo, a refuge for large birds of prey, black storks, eagles, vultures, and many other endangered species. Its landscape consists of extensive pastures or plains with rich seasonal grasslands, while the mountains and mountain ranges provide shelter for a wide variety of birds and mammals. Its magnificent natural setting invites all kinds of nature-related activities.

Porzuna was a Roman and Visigoth settlement, and it is from these civilizations that historical remains have been found. The first settlers of the region are probably from the Lower and Middle Paleolithic according to the archaeological remains found. Most of these can be found in the Ciudad Real museum.
A small town on the CR-7121 road towards Los Cortijos. The path starts to go up, so be prepared. Do not forget to visit the small Hermitage of Santa Teresa on the hill above.
Very smooth ascent of more than 5.5 km with an average difference in level of 3%. Follow the wind farms on the ridge.
